title: OperationalDNA
description:
A complete Operational DNA document for a business. Operational DNA describes organizational modeling — what the org is and what it does, independent of UI, API, or deployment technology. Modeled around the Actor > Action > Subject triad: Roles act, Subjects (Resource | Person | Role | Group) receive actions.

Organized in three categories:
- People: Person, Role, Group, Membership
- Entities: Resource, Attribute, Relationship
- Activities: Operation, Task, Step, Process, Trigger, Rule

Domain wraps the noun primitives (Resources, Persons, Roles, Groups). Memberships and Activities live at the top level of the document.

domain.description: The root domain, containing the full nested hierarchy of subdomains and noun primitives (Resources, Persons, Roles, Groups).

memberships.description: Template-level eligibility statements: which Person types may hold which Roles in which Groups.

operations.description: All Subject.Action operation pairs defined in this domain — the atomic units of business activity. Subject is any noun primitive (Resource, Person, Role, Group).

triggers.description: What initiates each Operation or Process — user actions, schedules, webhooks, chained operations.

rules.description: Constraints on operations — who may perform them (access rules) and what conditions must be satisfied (condition rules). Condition rules are also referenced by Step.conditions[] for compositional gating.

relationships.description: Named, directed connections between noun primitives — formalizes the semantic link a reference Attribute implies, adding cardinality.

tasks.description: Atomic reusable units of business activity: a Role performing exactly one Operation.

processes.description: Standard Operating Procedures — named, owned DAGs of Steps that accomplish business goals. Each Process declares an explicit `startStep` (Amazon-States-Language convention).
